title: Alkylations in Dimethylformamide
author: A. K. Sen
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The usual preparative procedure of  N -phthalyl DL- \(\beta\) -phenylalanine involves a reac­tion between phenylalanine and phthalic anhydride. One of the classical methods for making  N -phthalamido acids and free amino acids therefrom utilising Gabriel reaction is due to S \(\ddot{o}\) resen 1 . Our present  - work is essentially based on the S \(\ddot{o}\) resen's scheme, but the preparative procedure have bean significantly improved.
steps_raw: The usual preparative procedure of  N -phthalyl DL- \(\beta\) -phenylalanine involves a reac­tion between phenylalanine and phthalic anhydride. One of the classical methods for making  N -phthalamido acids and free amino acids therefrom utilising Gabriel reaction is due to S \(\ddot{o}\) resen 1 . Our present  - work is essentially based on the S \(\ddot{o}\) resen's scheme, but the preparative procedure have bean significantly improved.
